title: Mitomycin C inactivation of mouse embryonic fibroblasts (MEFs) for hPSC cultures
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"afhbg","text":"This protocol describes the process of using Mitomycin C to inactivate mouse embryonic fibroblasts (MEFs), which can then be used as feeder cells for human pluripotent stem cell (hPSC) culture.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c2i9a","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ekk9e","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c7mlv","text":"General notes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7gb51","text":"1. Throughout this protocol, the term hPSC is used to collectively refer to both hiPSCs and hESCs. All described procedures have been tested and work equally well for hiPSCs and hESCs.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ecqv7","text":"2. MEFs were obtained as described in Manipulating the Mouse Embryo: A Laboratory Manual, Third Edition (ISBN: 0879695919)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"80rqh","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"dt1tb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1r8lg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"86uh6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"citation","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"49619F46341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","title":"Manipulating the Mouse Embryo: A Laboratory Manual, 3rd ed.","protocol_guid":"","journal":"Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory Press","date":null,"created_on":"2026-02-10T22:33:37Z","doi":"","authors":"Andras Nagy, Marina Gertsenstein, Kristina Vintersten, \u0026 Richard Behringer","can_edit":false}}}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"7gh1m","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5f5lv","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"cc5bv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"","data":{"cellsMeta":{},"colTitles":["Item","Vendor","Catalog #"],"colWidths":["260",100,100],"data":[["DMEM","Corning","10-013-CV"],["FB Essence","Avantor","10803-034"],["FBS","Gibco","10437028"],["200mM L-Glutamine","Sigma","G8540"],["Penicillin \u0026 Streptomycin","Gibco","15140-122"],["MEM Non-Essential Amino Acids","Gibco","11140-050"],["Gelatin powder","Sigma","G2625"],["0.25% Trypsin/EDTA (Trypsin)","Gibco","25200-056"],["DPBS w/o Ca \u0026 Mg (DPBS)","Corning","MT21031CV"],["50ml centrifuge tubes","Corning","1495949A"],["10cm petri dish","Fisher","08757100D"],["10ml serological pipet","Corning","7200574"],["15cm tissue culture dish","Corning","0877224"]],"guid":"8132BDD07D9811ECB0AEC528C800364D","isJexcelDataFormat":true,"legend":"","mergeCells":{},"printData":[["DMEM","Corning","10-013-CV"],["FB Essence","Avantor","10803-034"],["FBS","Gibco","10437028"],["200mM L-Glutamine","Sigma","G8540"],["Penicillin \u0026 Streptomycin","Gibco","15140-122"],["MEM Non-Essential Amino Acids","Gibco","11140-050"],["Gelatin powder","Sigma","G2625"],["0.25% Trypsin/EDTA (Trypsin)","Gibco","25200-056"],["DPBS w/o Ca \u0026 Mg (DPBS)","Corning","MT21031CV"],["50ml centrifuge tubes","Corning","1495949A"],["10cm petri dish","Fisher","08757100D"],["10ml serological pipet","Corning","7200574"],["15cm tissue culture dish","Corning","0877224"]],"rowHeights":[23,23,23,23,23,23,23,23,23,23,23,23,23]}}}}
steps: Grow MEFs to 90-95% confluency in 15-cm cell culture dish
Aspirate the medium and add 15 ml of Mitomycin C solution to cover the surface
Aspirate Mitomycin C solution from the plates and wash 4 times with DPBS (with Ca/Mg) and final wash with DPBS (w/o Ca/Mg).
Add Trypsin and incubate in 37°C; 5% CO2 for 00:05:00
Add MEF medium to neutralize the Trypsin and collect the solution into a conical tube.
Centrifuge the cell suspension at 250 x g, Room temperature, 10 minutes
Discard supernatant and re-suspend the cells in fresh MEF medium
Count cells using trypan blue solution

Mitomycin C treated cells can be either freshly plated as feeder cells for hPCS cultures or frozen as Mitomycin C inactivated stocks at 10x10 6 cells/vial. A protocol on freezing MEFs can be found in the collection "Maintenance and inactivation of mouse embryonic fibroblasts (MEFs) as feeder cells for human pluripotent stem cell culture." A link to this collection can be found in the title section of this protocol, located above

MEF medium A B 1 DMEM 435 ml 2 FB Essence/FBS* 75 ml 3 200mM L-Glutamine 5 ml 4 Penicillin & Streptomycin (100x) 5 ml 5 MEM Non-Essential Amino Acids 5 ml *We have successfully used either FB Essence or FBS and have not observed an obvious difference. Final volume: 500ml
creator: Hanqin Li
